rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unbounded strcpy at line 9 copying argv data into buf[256] and explained that attacker-controlled command-line input can exceed the buffer, causing a stack-buffer-overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They only suggested strncpy/snprintf truncation without rejecting overlong input before copying, even though they ensured null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed eliminating fixed-size buffers and using dynamic allocation or a bounded/safe string API with centralized policies, removing raw unbounded string handling from the data path.
